title: Fuel Pressure Diagnostic
description: Diagnoses P0087 and fuel delivery faults by comparing live fuel rail pressure against commanded target using OBD-II and OEM-specific PIDs. Covers both traditional port injection and modern GDI (Gasoline Direct Injection) high-pressure systems with their distinctly different pressure ranges.
common_symptoms: P0087 fuel rail pressure too low
P00C6 low pressure fuel injector control
physical_solution: Connect scan tool and stream fuel rail pressure PIDs. Compare against commanded/target pressure. For port injection: target 40-60 psi at idle, drops at WOT indicate pump weakness. For GDI: low-pressure circuit 50-80 psi (LPFP), high-pressure circuit 1500-3000 psi (HPFP) at WOT. Install mechanical gauge at fuel rail Schrader valve for confirmation. For GDI: LP circuit testable with gauge, HP circuit dangerous — use scan tool only.

uds_services: $22 ReadDataByIdentifier — OEM fuel rail pressure DID
$22 ReadDataByIdentifier — OEM fuel pressure target DID
technical_solution.elm327_feasibility: Partial — Mode $01 PID $0A (fuel rail pressure absolute, 3 kPa/bit, range 0-765 kPa) accessible via ELM but limited resolution for GDI systems. OEM-specific high-resolution fuel pressure DIDs ($22) accessible on pre-gateway vehicles. GDI HP pressure typically via OEM DID only. Target/actual comparison requires OEM DID — ELM Mode $01 gives actual only.
preconditions: scan tool capable of streaming multiple PIDs simultaneously

procedure_steps.description: Stream Mode $01 PID $0A (01 0A) — fuel rail pressure. Calculation: A * 3 kPa. Convert: kPa * 0.145 = psi. Note: PID $0A is relative to vacuum (manifold referenced) for return-type systems.

Request OEM fuel rail pressure DID via $22 at ECM — Toyota DID 0x1201 (kPa), BMW DID 0x2F20 (low pressure) and 0x2F21 (high pressure), VW DID 0x0192 (injection pressure), Ford via enhanced PID. Compare actual vs DID commanded target value.

Port injection test procedure — start engine cold, record pressure at idle (40-60 psi target). Turn key off — pressure should hold above 35 psi for 30+ minutes (pressure regulator check). If immediate drop: leaking injector or check valve.
GDI low-pressure test — monitor LP fuel pressure DID at idle (target 50-80 psi). Blip throttle — LP should stay above 40 psi. If LP drops during blip: LPFP (in-tank) weak. P008A confirms LPFP issue.

GDI high-pressure test — monitor HP rail pressure DID during WOT acceleration. Target: 1500-3000 psi (varies by OEM and RPM). HPFP output correlates with RPM — high pressure only achievable when cam-driven pump spinning fast enough.

Compare fuel pressure at idle, part load, and WOT. If pressure below target at all conditions: check LPFP first. If LPFP OK but HPFP low: replace HPFP (reference high_pressure_fuel_pump.json). Clear DTCs and verify P0087 resolved.

oem_notes.Toyota/Lexus: GDI system on 2AR-FXE/2GR-FXS/8AR-FTS. LP circuit target 60-70 psi. HP circuit 363-2175 psi (varies with RPM). DID 0x1201 at ECM 0x700 for fuel pressure. Port injection on older models: 44-50 psi. HPFP on 2AR-FXE: cam-lobe driven, known for ticking noise.
oem_notes.Honda/Acura: GDI on 1.5T/2.0T/3.5L VTEC Turbo. LP: 60 psi. HP: 500-2900 psi depending on load. DID via HDS enhanced. Port injection on older models: 47-54 psi. HPFP on 2.0T: known for low-pressure fault at high altitude or with E10 fuel.
oem_notes.Nissan/Infiniti: Direct injection on VR30DDTT (Infiniti), MR20DD, KR20DDET. Port injection on QR25DE, VQ35DE: 51 psi. DI systems: LP 50-80 psi, HP 1450-2900 psi. CONSULT PID for HP fuel pressure. VQ37VHR has port injection — check $0A via ELM.
oem_notes.Ford: GDI on EcoBoost 1.5T/2.0T/2.7T/3.5T. Port+Direct injection (PFDI) on 5.0L Coyote Gen 3. LP: 50-65 psi. HP EcoBoost: 200-2175 psi. Enhanced PID [FORD_HPFP_DID] for HP pressure. P0087 on EcoBoost often HPFP cam follower wear.
oem_notes.GM: GDI on 1.5T/2.0T Ecotec, 5.3L/6.2L LT1. Port on 5.3L L83 (AFM). LP: 55-65 psi. HP: 1450-2900 psi. Enhanced PID at ECM for HP pressure. P0087 on 2.0T common from high-pressure pump failure.
oem_notes.BMW: B46/B48/B58 GDI. LP: 60-72 psi (supplied by in-tank LPFP). HP: DDE controls up to 3625 psi. DID 0x2F20 LP pressure, 0x2F21 HP pressure at ECM 0x12. N54/N55: HPFP 2900 psi max — known early failure pattern. ZGW blocks extended on F/G-series.
oem_notes.VW/Audi: EA888 2.0T GDI. LP: 72 psi. HP: 580-2175 psi. DID 0x0192 fuel injection pressure at ECM 0x01. Known HPFP cam follower wear on early 2.0T TSI — follower inspection at 30k miles. J fuel pressure sensor DTC 17964/18665. Pre-SFD ELM accessible.
oem_notes.Stellantis: GDI on 2.0T Hurricane, 2.4L MultiAir. Port injection on 5.7L/6.4L HEMI. HEMI: 46-58 psi at idle. Hurricane GDI LP: 65 psi, HP: up to 2175 psi. Enhanced PID via wiTECH for HP pressure. 2018+ SGW blocks extended.
oem_notes.Hyundai/Kia: GDI on 2.0T Theta II, 2.5L Smartstream, 3.3T Lambda. LP: 65 psi. HP: 725-2175 psi. DID [HKG_FUEL_PRES_DID] at ECM. Theta II 2.0T/2.4T: known LSPI (Low Speed Pre-Ignition) concern at high HP fuel pressure — check for oil dilution.
